title: Soft Token Alignment for Cross-Lingual Reasoning
abstract: arXiv:2606.26466v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Multilingual large language models often produce inconsistent reasoning and answers for semantically equivalent prompts in different languages. Prior work suggests that intermediate representations can be relatively language-agnostic, but generation becomes increasingly language-specific as models commit to discrete output tokens. This is problematic because language-specific lexical choices can cause semantically equivalent reasoning paths to diverge across languages. These divergences motivate searching for a cross-lingual alignment signal that is less tied to any single vocabulary item or script. We propose SOLAR, an auxiliary objective for supervised fine-tuning that aligns soft-token representations across languages, using English as a pivot. Soft tokens are probability-weighted mixtures over the vocabulary embeddings, yielding continuous representations that can aggregate information from semantically related tokens across language

Title: Soft Token Alignment for Cross-Lingual Reasoning
Abstract: arXiv:2606.26466v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Multilingual large language models often produce inconsistent reasoning and answers for semantically equivalent prompts in different languages. Prior work suggests that intermediate representations can be relatively language-agnostic, but generation becomes increasingly language-specific as models commit to discrete output tokens. This is problematic because language-specific lexical choices can cause semantically equivalent reasoning paths to diverge across languages. These divergences motivate searching for a cross-lingual alignment signal that is less tied to any single vocabulary item or script. We propose SOLAR, an auxiliary objective for supervised fine-tuning that aligns soft-token representations across languages, using English as a pivot. Soft tokens are probability-weighted mixtures over the vocabulary embeddings, yielding continuous representations that can aggregate information from semantically related tokens across language
translated_title: 软令牌对齐用于跨语言推理
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26466v1 公告类型：新  摘要：多语言大语言模型在处理不同语言中语义等价的提示时，常常会产生不一致的推理和答案。先前的研究表明，中间表示可以相对语言无关，但随着模型承诺于离散的输出令牌，生成过程变得越来越具有语言特异性。这带来了问题，因为语言特定的词汇选择会导致语义等价的推理路径在不同语言中产生分歧。这些分歧促使我们寻找一种与任何单一词汇项或书写系统关联较弱的跨语言对齐信号。我们提出了SOLAR，一种用于监督微调的辅助目标，它通过使用英语作为枢轴，跨语言对齐软令牌表示。软令牌是词汇嵌入上的概率加权混合，提供了能够跨语言聚合来自语义相关令牌信息的连续表示。
tldr: 本文提出SOLAR方法，通过软令牌对齐和英语枢轴，有效解决了多语言大模型在跨语言推理中因词汇差异导致的语义等价推理路径不一致问题。
en_tdlr: This paper proposes SOLAR, which uses soft token alignment with English as a pivot to effectively address the inconsistency in semantically equivalent reasoning paths across languages caused by lexical differences in multilingual large language models.
